sections.paragraph:
a root not used in Kal.-

(1) to hold, to hold fast, i.q. חָזַק, הֶחֱזִיק, see אָפִיק No. 1 , and Hithpael.

(2) to be strong, mighty, see אָפִיק No. 2 . For the signification of holding, especially holding firmly is often applied to strength. Arab. أَفَقَ is to overcome, to conquer; أَفِقَ to excel (prop. to be very strong) in liberality, eloquence; آفِقُ excellent, surpassing.

Hithpael, to hold oneself fast, to restrain oneself from giving way to the impulses of love, Gen 43:31 45:1 grief, Isa 42:14 anger, Est 5:10 conscience, 1Sa 13:12. Gen 45:1, “and Joseph could no longer restrain himself;” Isa 63:15, רַחֲמֶיךָ אֵלַי הִתְאַפָּֽקוּ “thy love towards me restrains itself;” 1 Sa. loc. cit. of Saul, “I forced myself, and offered the burnt offering” (although I knew that I was forbidden so to do). Besides the derivatives which immediately follow, see אָפִיק .
